programme.title: Inside Britain's National Parks

programme.desc: A collection of Elaine Paige's personal favourites from the BBC's song and dance archives, including songs from `Cats', `Sunset Boulevard', `My Fair Lady', `West Side Story' and `The Sound of Music'.

programme.title: The Capture

programme.title: In Our Blood: The Forever Chemicals Scandal
programme.desc: In almost every respect, Bentham is a quintessential Yorkshire town: proud, close-knit and set against breathtaking countryside. Two years ago, an environmental journalist's investigation revealed it to be the most contaminated place ever recorded in the UK for PFAS - toxic so-called forever chemicals, some of which are linked to cancers and other serious illnesses. For decades, the town's main employer, Angus Fire, produced firefighting foam now believed to be the likely source of the pollution. The company routinely manufactured and tested this foam on site; the government ultimately banned it in 2025. This groundbreaking documentary reveals how those chemicals may have spread beyond the factory boundary and how a small town is coming to terms with what it means for them.

programme.desc: Cases of murder-suicide are rare, but the sleepy town of Wilmslow saw two in the space of three years in the 1990s - both were elderly married couples found dead in their beds, and both crime scenes bore startling similarities. Police concluded that the husbands had killed their wives and then taken their own lives in both cases - as Sunday Times journalist David Collins reveals how he first broke the story after being leaked a police report into the Wilmslow murder-suicides, two former senior coroner's officers who tried to raise concerns over the deaths reveal for the first time why they believe there could be a miscarriage of justice. Using all available evidence, world-leading forensic scientist Dr Angela Gallop and renowned forensic pathologist Dr Dick Shephard take a fresh look at the cases to try to get closer to the truth.

programme.title: Premier League Stories
programme.desc: With exclusive behind-the-scenes access, PL Stories spends time with Yakubu Aiyegbeni at Finchley Boxing Club, where he is forging a new path in the next chapter of his sporting life. We explore his growing passion for boxing and the discipline it demands, offering a fresh perspective on life after football. Alongside this new pursuit, Yakubu reflects on his remarkable journey from his early years in Nigeria to becoming one of the Premier League's most prolific and respected strikers.
